name: Serralada Marina, Ca l'Alemany, Torre La Coscollada - circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Badalona offers diverse terrain, from coastal paths to the moderate inclines of the Parc de la Serralada de Marina. This natural park, characterized by Mediterranean forests of oak and pine, provides a network of trails. The region's topography ranges from sea level to hills reaching over 400 meters, ensuring varied riding experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Badalona The most popular mountain bike trail is Serralada Marina, Ca l'Alemany, Torre La Coscollada - circular, a 12.2 miles (19.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the heart of the Serralada de Marina. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Lion Fountain – La Coscollada Watchtower loop from Badalona Pompeu Fabra, a difficult 12.7 miles (20.4 km) path. This loop offers views from the watchtower and challenging ascents. Local mountain bikers also love the Pont del Petroli – Montgat loop from Artigues | Sant Adrià, a 20.4 miles (32.8 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 1 hour 51 minutes. Mountain biking around Badalona is defined by coastal paths, Mediterranean forests, and moderate inclines of the Serralada de Marina. The trail network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Badalona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1,500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Badalona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails are available around Badalona?
A: Badalona offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, from coastal paths along the Mediterranean to the varied topography of the Parc de la Serralada de Marina. You'll find routes through Mediterranean forests of oak and pine, gentle slopes, and more challenging ascents and descents. The region also features enduro-style riding with singletrack trails, some including berms and jumps.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Badalona?
A: Yes, there are trails suitable for various skill levels. While many routes in the Parc de la Serralada de Marina involve moderate to difficult climbs, you can find easier options, particularly along the coastal areas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32349986" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont del Petroli – Montgat loop from Artigues | Sant Adrià&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 32.8 km trail that explores the coastal scenery.

Q: Where can I find challenging mountain bike routes in Badalona?
A: For more challenging rides, focus on the Parc de la Serralada de Marina. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950547217/serralada-marina-ca-l-alema ny-torre-la-coscollada-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serralada Marina, Ca l'Alemany, Torre La Coscollada - circular&lt;/a&gt; (19.6 km with 510m elevation gain) or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32558369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lion Fountain – La Coscollada Watchtower loop from Badalona Pompeu Fabra&lt;/a&gt; (20.4 km with 498m elevation gain) offer significant ascents and technical sections. The region also has enduro-style singletrack with jumps and drops for advanced riders.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Badalona are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950547217/serralada-marina-ca-l-alema ny-torre-la-coscollada-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serralada Marina, Ca l'Alemany, Torre La Coscollada - circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32558369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lion Fountain – La Coscollada Watchtower loop from Badalona Pompeu Fabra&lt;/a&gt;, both offering comprehensive tours of the natural park.

Q: What are the best viewpoints or landmarks to see while mountain biking in Badalona?
A: The region offers several notable landmarks and viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5767347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont del Petroli&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic coastal views. In the Parc de la Serralada de Marina, the La Coscollada Watchtower offers expansive vistas. You can also pass by historical sites like the Monastery of Sant Jeroni de la Murtra or the Masia Can Miravitges, a medieval manor house.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Badalona?
A: The mountain biking trails around Badalona are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful Mediterranean forests, and the challenging yet rewarding ascents within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that include coastal scenery?
A: Absolutely. Badalona's location on the Mediterranean coast means you can combine mountain biking with sea views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32349986" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont del Petroli – Montgat loop from Artigues | Sant Adrià&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, offering a 32.8 km ride along the coast, passing by the iconic Pont del Petroli.

Q: Are there any longer mountain bike routes for endurance riders?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19018222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Amigó – Cornice Viewpoint loop from Badalona Pompeu Fabra&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 40.3 km route with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain, providing a significant test of endurance and skill.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Badalona?
A: Badalona generally enjoys a pleasant climate, making mountain biking enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures, ideal for longer rides. Even in winter, the climate is often suitable, though some higher sections in the Parc de la Serralada de Marina might be cooler. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during peak season.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or parks accessible by mountain bike?
A: The primary natural attraction for mountain bikers is the Parc de la Serralada de Marina, Badalona's 'green lung.' This natural park features extensive Mediterranean forests and diverse terrain. Within the city, Parc de Can Solei i Ca l'Arnús also offers a green space, though trails there are typically less rugged.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites?
A: Yes, some routes in the Parc de la Serralada de Marina will take you near historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38220161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lion Fountain – Hermitage of Saint Climent loop from Badalona Pompeu Fabra&lt;/a&gt; passes by the Hermitage of Saint Climent. The 15th-century Gothic Monastery of Sant Jeroni de la Murtra is also nestled within the natural park and can be a point of interest on longer rides.
tips: Main entrance to Serralada de Marina Park from the Montigalà neighborhood of the municipality of Badalona. Very busy on weekends.

Ca l'Alemany is an old farmhouse in the Canyet de Badalona neighborhood, located in the Betlem or San Jeroni valley, near the Turó de Montigalà, halfway between the city, marked by the B-20, and the Sant Jeroni de la Murtra monastery.

   Currently only ruins remain, the best preserved part of which are the laundry room in the courtyard and the arches that accompany it. Despite its status, it is currently protected as a Cultural Asset of Local Interest.

   An archaeological excavation confirmed the presence of a previous Roman villa, which would later have given way to the farmhouse. Despite its state, it is still a characteristic element of the landscape of the Sant Jeroni valley.

The Ca l'Alemany aqueduct (Badalona) is located near the Sant Jeroni de la Murtra monastery. It was built in the late 19th or early 20th century. This aqueduct carried the water to the Ca l'Alemany farmhouse, currently in ruins.

   An underground pipeline transported the water from the Ca l'Alemany mine to a small shed where it was sent to the farmhouse. Currently this aqueduct preserves its structure of six stone arches.

One of the most emblematic viewpoints of the Serralada de Marina is the Mirador del Collet de la Vallençana, which is located in the valley of Vallensana, at the intersection of the BV-5011 road from Badalona to Montcada i Reixac with Camí Vell street in Montcada

   It can be accessed by public transport with the B12 bus line. This viewpoint has benches, trees and trash cans, to rest and enjoy the views over Canyet and the eastern sector of the Parque de la Serralada de Marina.

Emblematic crossroads and meeting point for cyclists and hikers, where the main track that goes up from the Mirador de la Vallençana towards the Turó La Coscollada converge, with the trail that runs parallel to the quarry from the Turó de Les Maleses and with the path that goes down to the Font de l'Amigó.
It is a common point in the area, of arrival and passage of different mountain biking, hiking and running routes, where the views are impressive.

waypoints.name: Montigalà Entrance to Parc Serralada Marina
